item.title: Index of 25 Proxy Errors (+Exact Fixes For Each)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
How to Fix 25 Common Proxy Issues Whether you’re using proxies for web scraping, automation, or simply browsing the internet privately, encountering proxy errors is an inevitable fact of life. But which proxy errors are most common? Why do they happen, and what should you do about them? The only way to find out is […]

The post Index of 25 Proxy Errors (+Exact Fixes For Each) appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
How to Fix 25 Common Proxy Issues

Whether you’re using proxies for web scraping, automation, or simply browsing the internet privately, encountering proxy errors is an inevitable fact of life.

But which proxy errors are most common? Why do they happen, and what should you do about them?

The only way to find out is to take a look inside…

Inside this troubleshooting index you’ll find the full list of 25 most common proxy errors, a detailed explanation of what causes each one, and the exact fix for rapidly getting back online.

Let’s take a closer look.

Decode Proxy Errors: The Easy Way

Proxy errors typically fall into one of three categories: authentication/configuration errors, connection/network errors, and proxy blocking/detection errors.

The solutions for each are very different, so it’s important to first determine which issue type you’re experiencing before trying random fixes that may or may not work.

Authentication & Configuration Errors

1. 407 Proxy Authentication Required Error

Meaning: You did not, or incorrectly entered, proxy credentials (IP authentication).

Fix:

  • Make sure your username/password are correctly configured
  • Check for stray spaces before/after credentials
  • Make sure you’re using Basic authentication, not Digest
  • Double check your credentials with proxy provider

2. Incorrect Proxy Format Error

Meaning: Your proxy IP is formatted incorrectly in your browser/app/script.

Fix:

  • Enter proxies in the following format: protocol://username:password@ip:port
  • e.g. : http://user123:pass456@123.45.67.89:8080
  • Ensure you URL encode special characters in password
  • Use HTTP, HTTPS or SOCKS5 (pick the right protocol)

3. Port Not Configured Error

Meaning: Port is blocked, not configured properly, or proxy server not listening on that port.

Fix:

  • Double-check which port your proxy service provider uses (default is 8080, but can also be 3128, 1080, etc.)
  • Ensure computer firewall isn’t blocking said port
  • Try different port numbers if possible (ask provider which ports they support)
  • Port must match proxy protocol (HTTP/S vs SOCKS5)

4. IP Not Whitelisted Error

Meaning: Current IP address is not authorized to use proxy service.

Fix:

  • Login to Ziny.io dashboard & whitelist current IP address
  • (username/pass authentication is recommended if IP’s frequently change)
  • If you have a static IP on your cloud server – whitelist server IP instead
  • Update whitelist whenever you change networks/locations

Connection & Network Errors

5. Connection Timed Out Error

Meaning: Proxy did not respond within an allotted amount of time.

Fix:

  • Increase timeout settings within your browser/app/script (start with 30-60 seconds)
  • Ping/Telnet test proxy IP to see if port is reachable
  • Rotate to a different proxy in your pool
  • Check if internet connection is down
  • Contact Support if timing out on every proxy/IP

6. Connection Refused Error

Meaning: Proxy server refused connection.

Fix:

  • Double-check proxy IP/port is correct
  • Whitelist current IP if using IP authentication
  • Ensure you have not exceeded connection limits
  • Try connecting with a different proxy to rule out problems
  • Verify your subscription hasn’t expired

7. DNS Lookup Failed Error

Meaning: Unable to resolve proxy hostname to an IP.

Fix:

  • Try entering proxy IP directly instead of hostname
  • Ensure your DNS settings are correct (use Google DNS 8.8.8.8 or Cloudflare 1.1.1.1)
  • Flush DNS Cache ( Windows: ipconfig /flushdns | Mac: sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder )
  • Check proxy hostname spelling is correct

8. SSL Handshake Failed Error

Meaning: SSL handshake failed when establishing an encrypted connection to proxy.

Fix:

  • Update SSL/TLS libraries to latest version
  • Turn off SSL verify (for testing purposes only!)
  • Check date/time is correct on your computer
  • Verify proxy supports SSL/TLS version you’re using
  • Switch from HTTPS proxy to HTTP proxy instead

Blocking & Detection Errors

9. 403 Forbidden Error

Meaning: Website recognized/blocklisted your proxy IP.

Fix:

  • Rotate proxy to a new, unblocked IP
  • Try using a residential or mobile proxy IP instead
  • Spoof realistic headers + user agent
  • Add random delay between requests
  • Ensure you’re not missing any required headers or cookies
  • Try scraping tougher websites with Ziny’s residential proxy network.

10. Proxy Blocked By Website Error

Meaning: Proxy was detected by website.

Fix:

  • Use higher quality residential proxies
  • Increase proxy rotation frequency
  • Spoof browser fingerprint
  • Consider using session proxies instead
  • Add realistic request headers such as Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, Referer, etc
  • Render JavaScript if website uses it for bot detection.

11. CAPTCHA Error

Meaning: Website detected suspicious activity and wants CAPTCHA solved.

Fix:

  • Slow down request speeds
  • Use CAPTCHA solving services like 2Captcha / Anti-Captcha
  • Try using a residential proxy IP instead
  • Rotate user agents and browser profiles
  • Add random delays in between requests (2-5 seconds)
  • Implement session persistence (stick with same proxy & cookies)

12. IP Blocked or Blacklisted Error

Meaning: Proxy’s IP is listed in a public or private blacklist.

Fix:

  • Rotate immediately to a different proxy IP
  • Check MXToolbox or Spamhaus to see if IP is listed on a known blacklist
  • Ask proxy provider for replacement IP if dedicated proxy.
  • If you are using a shared proxy – rotation will fix this issue.
  • Don’t scrape aggressively with the same IP.

Rate Limiting & Performance Errors

13. 429 Too Many Requests Error

Meaning: Sending too many requests in a short period of time.

Fix:

  • Implement exponential backoff – wait 1s, then 2s, then 4s, etc…
  • Rotate among more proxy IP addresses
  • Add random delay between requests (3-10 seconds)
  • Reduce max number of concurrent connections per domain
  • Look for “Retry-After” header in response
  • Check response headers for rate limit info.

14. Site Too Slow Error

Meaning: Proxy responded, but taking too long.

Fix:

  • Run a proxy speed test
  • Use proxies closer to website server
  • Reduce max concurrent connections per proxy
  • Ensure proxy provider has performance guarantees
  • Monitor if slow down happens at specific times/day
  • (i.e – try scraping only when website is in their OFF-peak hours based on timezone)
  • Upgrade to premium/multi tier proxies for important use cases.

15. Bandwidth Limit Reached Error

Meaning: You’ve exceeded your allocated bandwidth / data transfer allowance.

Fix:

  • Check bandwidth usage on Ziny.io dashboard
  • Upgrade plan if permanently running into limits
  • Optimize requests to use less bandwidth (compress responses, request less data)
  • Implement caching – don’t repeat requests that have already been done.
  • Monitor bandwidth usage & predict when you’ll hit the limit.

Protocol & Compatibility Errors

16. SOCKS vs HTTP Proxy Error

Meaning: Your app only works with one proxy protocol, but your proxy is configured otherwise.

Fix:

  • Make sure you know which proxy protocol your application supports. (HTTP/HTTPS? SOCKS4? SOCKS5?)
  • Configure proper proxy type in your app/browser settings.
  • Use HTTP proxies for general web browsing/applications.
  • Use SOCKS5 proxies if your app requires UDP or deeper packet protocol support.
  • Refer to Ziny.io documentation for which proxy protocols we support.

17. IPv4 vs IPv6 Error

Meaning: Application only works with one IP protocol version but proxy is configured with other.

Fix:

  • Check if website only supports IPv6.
  • Always use IPv4 proxies for best compatibility.
  • Set your computer/network to prefer IPv4 when both are available.
  • Check if network you’re on supports IP version your proxy uses.

18. Proxy Chain Error

Meaning: You are using 2+ proxies in a row, and one of them failed.

Fix:

  • Test each proxy individually to see which one isn’t working.
  • Proxy chaining is bad. Don’t do it unless absolutely necessary.
  • Ensure each proxy in your chain supports proxy forwarding.
  • Increase timeout settings to accommodate for chain delay.

Application Specific Errors

19. Proxy Settings Not Detected in Browser Error

Meaning: Browser/browser extension not applying proxy config.

Fix:

  • Clear cache and restart browser
  • Check if browser extensions are conflicting
  • Double check proxy settings are correct in browser
  • Test using whatismyip.com to see if proxy is detected
  • Attempt in incognito/private mode to rule out extension conflicts
  • Use a browser-specific proxy extension for easy setup.

20. Detected as Bot/Script Error

Meaning: Website detected your requests as coming from a bot/rpa/script.

Fix:

  • Rotate user agents regularly
  • Add natural mouse movement and click patterns. (Puppeteer / Playwright)
  • Add all default headers a browser would send.
  • Randomize time between each request.
  • Try using a residential proxy IP.
  • Render JavaScript
  • Use browser automation and proper stealth plugins. Don’t use heavyweight automation unless necessary.
  • Refer to this article to learn how to prevent bot detection.

21. Cookies Are Not Being Saved/Error Error

Meaning: Unable to persist session state when routing through proxy.

Fix:

  • Enable cookie persistence with HTTP client
  • Stick with the same proxy when using session/proxies (session proxies)
  • Store and replay cookies.
  • Handle sessions correctly in your code.
  • Check if website requires a special cookie to be set.
  • Check if proxy is stripping out cookie headers.

Proxy Pool & Management Errors

22. Proxy Pool Empty Error

Meaning: Ran out of proxies in your proxy pool.

Fix:

  • Increase size of your proxy pool
  • Automatically test proxy health & remove unhealthy from rotation.
  • Implement cool-down periods for proxies after they get blocked.
  • Remove hard-banned IPs from proxy rotation.
  • Upgrade to a larger proxy package.
  • Utilize Ziny’s automatic proxy rotation.

23. Inconsistent Proxy Quality Error

Meaning: Success-rate drastically fluctuates between proxies.

Fix:

  • Implement scoring mechanism to rank proxies by success-rate.
  • Remove bad proxies from active rotation.
  • Always test proxies before adding them into production pool.
  • Monitor proxy metrics (uptime/speed/success-rate, etc.)
  • Upgrade to Ziny’s premium proxy packages for consistent proxy quality.
  • Utilize Ziny’s quality-filtered proxy pools.

24. Proxy Lease Expired Error

Meaning: Proxy subscription/session has expired.

Fix:

  • Check your Ziny.io dashboard to ensure you’re still subscribed.
  • Setup auto renewal so this doesn’t happen.
  • Manually monitor expiration dates if using dedicated proxies.
  • Implement retry logic to handle errors gracefully.
  • Keep billing info up to date.

Prevent Proxy Errors Before They Happen

Of course the best solution to proxy errors is prevention. Implement these best practices, and you’ll drastically reduce your chances of experiencing any of the 25 common proxy errors listed above:

Handle errors: Code defensively by gracefully handling proxy errors – retry requests, switch to a backup proxy, log errors, and throw them up the chain for manual inspection.

Monitor proxy health: Automatically monitor proxy uptime and success-rate. Remove bad proxies from rotation and never let poor quality proxies affect your production use-cases.

Use the right proxies: Use the correct proxy type for your use-case. Datacenter proxies for highest performance. Residential proxies for bypassing anti-scraping technology. Mobile proxies for mobile automation.

Be respectful: Don’t set your concurrency too high. Scale slow. Honor websites’ robots.txt. Don’t overload servers with requests. Many blocks occur due to disrespecting target websites.

Keep configs up-to-date: Update whitelisted IPs, proxy configurations, and authentication credentials periodically. Most block/issues occur from outdated info.

Use Ziny’s proxy features: Leverage Ziny.io’s features like automatic proxy rotation, geo-targeting, and quality-filtered proxy pools.

Conclusion

Don’t let proxy errors stress you out. Learn why they happen, how to fix them, and take the proper steps to prevent them from happening in the first place.

Each proxy error has an easy fix. Use this troubleshooting index next time you encounter that dreaded 403 error, or get blocked by a website saying “proxy blocked”.

With Ziny’s world-class proxy solutions, you’ll experience fewer proxy errors and smoother performance. Give us a try today!

The post Index of 25 Proxy Errors (+Exact Fixes For Each) appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: Residential vs ISP vs Mobile Proxies: What You Actually Need in 2026
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
Residential, ISP and Mobile Proxies Compared You run 50 Instagram accounts for clients. One day you check and realize 30 of them have been banned overnight. Bad luck? Wrong proxy choice. 2026 has seen the entry of actors in every market who offer proxies that are terrible and expensive or overkill for your use case. […]

The post Residential vs ISP vs Mobile Proxies: What You Actually Need in 2026 appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
Residential, ISP and Mobile Proxies Compared

You run 50 Instagram accounts for clients. One day you check and realize 30 of them have been banned overnight. Bad luck? Wrong proxy choice.

2026 has seen the entry of actors in every market who offer proxies that are terrible and expensive or overkill for your use case. The key to succeeding long term is using proxies matched to technical capabilities to your specific requirements.

In this guide we’ll compare residential, ISP, and mobile proxies including actual pricing, performance metrics, and a decision framework for buying the right proxy type for you.

The Three Proxy Types in a Nutshell

Residential Proxies: Powered by real home internet connections

Residential proxies work by routing your requests through IP addresses assigned to physical homes by internet service providers (ISPs). When you make a request, it passes through the proxy and the destination sees a real residential user connecting from their home in Manchester, Toronto, or Tokyo.

Flow:

Your device → Proxy server → Residential user’s device → Target website

Major characteristics:

IP pool size: 10M-200M+ addresses

Geographic targeting at city level for 200+ countries

Rotation: Automatic or sticky sessions

Speed range: 2-10 Mbps (varying by end-user connections)

Trust score: High (actual residential IPs)

Residential proxies are great for the vast majority of use cases that require large IP diversity and geographic reach. However, the speed varies a lot because they go through the end-user connection which is variable.

ISP Proxies: Combining speed of datacenters with residential trust

ISP proxies (also known as static residential proxies) use IPs hosted in datacenters but registered to internet service provider autonomous system numbers (ASN). This gives you a great middle ground of datacenter infrastructure speed and the trust of residential IP registration.

Flow:

Your device → Datacenter server (with ISP ASN) → Target website

Major characteristics:

IP pool size: thousands instead of millions

Rotation: Static IPs that do not rotate unless you switch

Speed range: 50-100 Mbps (datacenter infrastructure)

Uptime: 99%+ (depends on hosting server reliability)

Trust score: High (most platforms trust these as residential)

ISP proxies are the middle ground between residential proxies and datacenter IPs in the best way possible. You give up massive IP diversity for blazing fast static IPs.

Mobile Proxies: Maximum anonymity

Mobile proxies route your traffic through mobile networks and carriers like Verizon, T-Mobile, or Vodafone. Each mobile network uses a carrier-grade NAT (CGNAT) that allows 100s-1000s of other users to share the same IP addresses. This makes it nearly impossible to track the real user.

Flow:

Your device → Mobile device/carrier network → Target website

Major characteristics:

IP pool size: Limited by carrier inventory

Rotation: Automatic but controlled by the carriers, not you

Speed range: 1-10 Mbps (depends on network quality)

Shared IPs between 100-1000+ users at a time

Trust score: Maximum (carrier IPs never get blocked)

Mobile proxies should be thought of as the big guns you use when detection leads to project failure. They are correspondingly expensive.

Proxy Performance: Speed, Cost, and Reliability Comparison

Speed and Stability

Speed is the only metric where ISP proxies win in a big way. For tasks where speed is non-negotiable like sneaker drops or scraping SERPs very quickly, ISP proxies are necessary.

Stability-wise, datacenter hosting of ISP proxies gives you much less disconnection in general compared to both residential and mobile proxies.

Cost Breakdown

Residential proxies:

Budget tier: $3-7 per GB (Ziny starts at $4/GB)

Mid-tier: $7-15 per GB

Premium: $15-30 per GB

Ideal for: If your projects use 50GB+ monthly

ISP proxies:

Budget tier: $1.50-3 per IP/month

Mid-tier: $3-8 per IP/month

Premium: $8-20 per IP/month

Ideal for: If your projects use 10-50 IPs

Mobile proxies:

Budget tier: $10-20 per GB

Mid-tier: $20-40 per GB

Premium: $40-80 per GB

Ideal for: If speed is crucial and cost is secondary

Let’s look at a real example. Scraping 100,000 pages per month can take 20-30GB of residential bandwidth (costing $60-450 with providers like Ziny) or 10-15 ISP proxies ($15-120). Mobile proxies would cost $200-600 for the same task which is fine when residential proxies are failing but not otherwise.

Detection Risk

Mobile proxies have the lowest risk because of CGNAT. When 1000s of legitimate users are sharing the same IP address, platforms risk blacklisting thousands of genuine users if they detect.

Residential proxies are next. Real IPs from real people that are very difficult to tell apart from non-proxy traffic.

ISP proxies are the rare exception that sometimes get flagged by very advanced systems that correlate the ASN numbers in public data with actual connections, but this is uncommon with good providers.

Use Cases: Which Proxy for Each Task?

Pick Residential Proxies When:

  1. Web Scraping at Scale Monitoring e-commerce prices, real estate data collection, travel site aggregation, or any project that needs 100+ unique IPs daily.

Use Case Example: A fashion retailer scrapes 500,000 products daily across 15 competitors and needs 200GB of residential bandwidth per month. The cost is $600-1,400/month. Enterprise alternative: $3,000+/month.

  1. SEO/SERP Tracking Search ranking tracking, competitor analysis, and keyword research needs geographic coverage that only residential proxies give.
  2. Ad Verification at Scale Want to check how ads display in different locations/demographics? Residential IPs allow for accurate verification.
  3. Multi-Account Management (Moderate Levels) Managing 10-50 social media accounts, marketplace seller accounts, or similar where IP diversity trumps individual IP speed.

Choose ISP Proxies When:

  1. High-Speed Scraping Scraping SERPs on Google, data-heavy APIs, or any scenario where millisecond-level response time matters. ISP proxies offer datacenter-level speeds without the datacenter reputation.

Use Case Example: An SEO firm manages 10,000 keywords on Google with 20 ISP proxies. Response time per keyword: 5 seconds. With residential proxies: 25+ seconds.

  1. E-commerce Operations Sneaker bots, event ticket purchases, and limited-release products that need speed to beat competitors.
  2. Small-Scale Multi-Account Management For 5-20 high-value accounts where consistent IP identity matters. Static nature prevents issues due to rotation.
  3. Long-Running Sessions When you need to maintain hours-long connections to the same site. ISP proxies do not randomly disconnect as residential proxies can.

Choose Mobile Proxies When:

  1. Large-Scale Social Media Management Instagram accounts in 100+ numbers, TikTok’s strict monitoring, or Facebook accounts that get blocked frequently. Mobile proxies make these tasks much more feasible.

Use Case Example: A marketing agency runs 500 Instagram accounts with mobile proxies. Monthly ban rate is <1%. With residential proxies: 15% monthly bans.

  1. Bypassing Advanced Anti-Bot Measures Sites with heavy CAPTCHA, Cloudflare challenges, or networks that block residential/datacenter IPs.
  2. Mobile App Testing App store visibility checks, mobile-only content scraping, or carrier-specific feature testing that requires genuine mobile traffic.
  3. Extreme Anonymity Requirements Operations so sensitive that any detection leads to total failure and you’re fine paying for mobile proxies.

Hybrid Approaches: Combining Proxy Types

Sophisticated operators leverage more than one proxy type.

Approach 1: Residential + ISP Mix Use residential proxies for general data discovery and scraping. Switch to ISP proxies for time-sensitive scraping or consistent identity requirements.

Savings: 40-60% lower cost than all-ISP or all-mobile.

Approach 2: Mobile for High Risk + Residential for Bulk Use mobile proxies for high-risk accounts (social media, ticket bots). Use residential proxies for scraping and research tasks.

ROI: Ideal balance, pay premium only where it prevents loss.

Approach 3: Tiered Account Management

1st Tier (Premium clients): Mobile

2nd Tier (Regular clients): ISP

3rd Tier (Testing/Low-risk): Residential

Match proxy pricing to client account value, optimize profitability.

Step-by-Step Implementation Guide

Decision Framework

Cost Examples: Real-World Budgeting

Small-Scale Operation (Freelancer/Small Agency):

5 ISP proxies + 20GB residential = $100-200/month (With Ziny’s residential proxies starting at $4/GB, this could be as low as $80-120/month)

Handles: 50,000 scraped pages + 10-15 accounts

Ideal for: Freelancers, small agencies, startups

Medium-Scale Operation (Mid-Sized Business):

150GB residential + 20GB mobile = $650-1,200/month

Handles: 500,000+ pages scraped + 100 social accounts

Ideal for: Established agencies, mid-sized e-commerce

Large-Scale Operation (Enterprise Level):

1TB residential + 200GB mobile + 50 ISP = $5,000-15,000/month

Handles: Multi-million pages scraped + 1000+ accounts

Potential ROI: 10-50x if data-driven decisions drive revenue

Provider Checklist

Residential Proxies: ✓ IP pool size: 10M+ addresses ✓ Geographic coverage: 150+ countries ✓ Ethical sourcing: Consent-based, transparent ✓ Rotation controls: Automatic and sticky session options ✓ Clear billing, no hidden fees/throttling

ISP Proxies: ✓ Uptime guarantee: 99%+ ✓ Multiple ASN options for rotation ✓ Support for easy IP rotation/replacement ✓ Transparent and tiered pricing

Mobile Proxies: ✓ Genuine carrier networks (verify 4G/5G) ✓ Confirmation of CGNAT architecture (not simulated) ✓ Geographic targeting options ✓ Comprehensive documentation/support

Ziny’s residential proxy network checks all these boxes with 30M+ IPs across 195+ countries.

Red flags for any provider:

Suspiciously low pricing (possible botnet)

No ethical sourcing information

Poor customer support or non-existent

Unclear refund/policies on trials

Finalizing Your Choice

Opt for residential proxies if:

You need >100 rotating IPs each day

Geographic targeting is important

Monthly budget: $200-1,000

Use case: Large-scale scraping, SEO, research

Opt for ISP proxies if:

Speed is the top priority

Fewer than 50 IPs are needed

Consistent IPs needed (no rotation)

Monthly budget: $50-300

Use case: Fast scraping, low account management

Opt for mobile proxies if:

Running 50+ social media accounts at scale

Dealing with aggressive bot detection

Extreme anonymity needed

Monthly budget: $500+ is acceptable

Use case: Instagram/TikTok, mobile apps, critical tasks

The best proxy type turns operations from headaches with bans into fast and smooth processes. Test with a 7-day trial, see actual success rates, then scale up based on performance.

In 2026, it’s no longer about the best proxy out there. It’s about the right proxy for your unique use case. Make the right choice and you’ll not only save money, you’ll be outperforming other operators using completely the wrong tools regardless of price.

Ready to Get Started?

Ziny offers all three proxy types with transparent pricing and no hidden fees:

– Residential Proxies: Starting at $4/GB with 30M+ IPs

– ISP Proxies: High-speed static IPs from $2.50/IP

– Mobile Proxies: Genuine 4G/5G networks across major carriers

Try Ziny risk-free with a 7-day trial and see the difference quality proxies make for your operations.

The post Residential vs ISP vs Mobile Proxies: What You Actually Need in 2026 appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: Choosing Proxies for Each Workflow: Scraping, SMM, Monitoring, Multi-Login (One Page Guide)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
The Proxy Selection Blueprint: Stop Wasting 60% of Your Budget on Wrong IPs If you’re using residential proxies for everything, you’re burning money. If you’re using datacenter proxies for social media management, you’re burning accounts. After testing 15+ proxy providers across four critical workflows, we have identified the exact proxy-to-task matches that maximize ROI while […]

The post Choosing Proxies for Each Workflow: Scraping, SMM, Monitoring, Multi-Login (One Page Guide) appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
The Proxy Selection Blueprint: Stop Wasting 60% of Your Budget on Wrong IPs

If you’re using residential proxies for everything, you’re burning money. If you’re using datacenter proxies for social media management, you’re burning accounts. After testing 15+ proxy providers across four critical workflows, we have identified the exact proxy-to-task matches that maximize ROI while minimizing blocks and bans.

This isn’t another generic “what is a proxy” article. This is the tactical playbook for choosing the right proxy type for web scraping, social media management, monitoring, and multi-account operations in 2026.

The Four Proxy Types: What Actually Matters

Datacenter Proxies are server-hosted IPs. Blazing fast, dirt cheap ($0.50-2/GB), but easily flagged by sophisticated anti-bot systems. Think of them as budget rental cars: great for short trips, terrible for impressing anyone.

Residential Proxies use real household IPs from actual ISPs. Trusted by websites, slower than datacenter, and premium-priced ($3-8/GB). These are your stealth vehicles as they blend in perfectly but cost significantly more.

ISP Proxies (static residential) combine datacenter speed with residential legitimacy. They’re registered with ISPs but hosted on servers. The sweet spot for stable, long-term sessions at $1-3/proxy/month.

Mobile Proxies route through 3G/4G/5G carriers. Maximum trust due to CGNAT (multiple users share IPs naturally), premium pricing ($50-150/proxy/month), and essential for mobile-first platforms. The luxury option when nothing else works.

Workflow #1: Web Scraping—Match Proxy to Target Security

Low-Security Targets: Datacenter Wins

Scraping public blogs, news sites, or basic e-commerce? Datacenter proxies deliver 5x the speed at 1/10th the cost of residential. These sites don’t invest in sophisticated bot detection—save your premium proxies for tougher targets.

Configuration: Rotate every 5-10 requests, pool of 100+ IPs, HTTP/HTTPS protocol.

Real savings: One client switched from residential to datacenter for scraping 50 news sites. Monthly cost dropped from $1,200 to $180 with zero impact on success rates.

Medium-Security Targets: Residential Required

Amazon, eBay, travel sites, and most major e-commerce platforms actively block datacenter IPs. Residential proxies become non-negotiable here.

Critical configuration: Rotate every 1-5 requests, use sticky sessions for pagination (5-10 minutes), and match proxy location to request headers. A Brazilian IP with English-only language preferences screams “bot.”

Success metric: Aim for >95% success rate. If you’re below 90%, your proxies aren’t the right fit.

High-Security Targets: Mobile + Residential Hybrid

Social platforms, financial sites, and protected APIs require mobile proxies for initial authentication, then residential for data collection. In our testing, mobile proxies reduced blocks by 70% compared to residential alone on Instagram and LinkedIn.

The hybrid approach: Use expensive mobile proxies strategically (authentication, account creation, high-value requests) and switch to residential for bulk operations. This reduced one client’s proxy spend from $3,400 to $1,200 monthly while maintaining success rates.

Workflow #2: Social Media Management—One Account, One Proxy, Forever

This is the non-negotiable rule that saves accounts: each social media account needs its own dedicated static proxy that never changes.

Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok track IP history aggressively. In our 90-day test managing 50 Instagram accounts, those using rotating proxies had a 60% suspension rate. Those using static ISP proxies? 0% suspensions.

The Right Setup

For Instagram/Facebook/TikTok:

  • 1 static ISP or residential proxy per account
  • 24-hour to 30-day sessions
  • Location matched to account’s registered location
  • SOCKS5 protocol preferred

For high-value accounts (100k+ followers, business accounts):

  • Upgrade to mobile proxies
  • $50-150/month is cheap insurance for a $50,000+ account

The Missing Piece: Anti-Detect Browsers

Perfect proxies fail without browser fingerprint management. Platforms track canvas fingerprints, WebGL, fonts, timezone, and 50+ other identifiers. Pair your proxies with Multilogin, AdsPower, or GoLogin to create unique browser profiles per account.

Real result: Adding anti-detect browsers to our proxy setup dropped account flags from 24% to 0% over three months.

Workflow #3: Monitoring & SEO—Accuracy Demands Precision

SERP Tracking: Location Is Everything

Google delivers different results based on city-level location, device type, and personalization. If you’re tracking “best pizza NYC” from a Chicago IP, your data is worthless.

Configuration: Residential proxies with city-level targeting, mobile proxies for mobile SERP tracking, minimal rotation (same IP per full session), and 3-5 IPs per location for statistical accuracy.

Cost optimization: Most businesses over-collect SERP data. Track rankings weekly, not daily, unless you’re in a volatile niche. This alone cuts monitoring costs 70%.

Ad Verification: Mobile Is Non-Negotiable

70% of digital ad impressions occur on mobile devices. Desktop proxies cannot verify mobile ad placement accurately. Allocate 60-70% of your ad verification budget to mobile proxies, 30-40% to residential for desktop verification.

Workflow #4: Multi-Account Management—Stability Wins

Managing multiple e-commerce stores, gaming accounts, or SaaS subscriptions requires rock-solid IP stability.

E-Commerce (Amazon, eBay, Shopify)

Rule: 1 dedicated static ISP proxy per seller account, same IP for 30-90 days minimum, location matching business address.

Critical mistake to avoid: Never log into multiple stores from the same IP, even on different platforms. Platforms share data. We’ve seen accounts suspended because they shared an IP with a banned account on an entirely different marketplace.

Sneaker Bots & Limited Releases

ISP proxies dominate here. Speed and low latency are critical during drops. Configuration: 1 proxy per bot task, geo-targeted to release location, refresh proxies 24 hours before major drops.

Provider insight: NetNut and Live Proxies consistently outperform others for sneaker bots due to direct ISP partnerships and superior US/UK coverage.

The Cost-Optimization Strategy Nobody Talks About

Here’s the secret: hybrid proxy strategies cut costs 40-60% without sacrificing success rates.

The Hybrid Model

Start cheap, escalate when blocked:

  1. Begin with datacenter for initial requests
  2. Monitor block rates in real-time
  3. Switch to residential only when datacenter fails
  4. Reserve mobile for critical failures

Implementation breakdown:

  • 70% datacenter (bulk, low-value requests)
  • 25% residential (medium security)
  • 5% mobile (high-value, blocked scenarios)

Real result: This approach reduced one SaaS client’s proxy spend from $2,400 to $950 monthly while scraping the same data volume.

Volume Negotiation Tactics

Once you’re using 100-200 GB monthly, negotiate aggressively. We’ve secured:

  • Smartproxy/Decodo: $3.50/GB → $2.20/GB (37% reduction)
  • NetNut: $3.45/GB → $2.80/GB (19% reduction)
  • Bright Data: 35% volume discounts

Script to use: “I’m currently with [Competitor] at [Price]. I’m scaling to [Higher Volume]. Can you beat their pricing?”

The Three Mistakes Destroying Your Success Rates

Mistake #1: Poor Header Configuration

Brazilian IP + Accept-Language: en-US = instant red flag.

Fix: Match all headers to proxy location. Use browser fingerprint randomization tools to ensure timezone, language, and geolocation align perfectly with your proxy’s country.

Mistake #2: Aggressive Rate Limiting

Platforms have invisible thresholds. Exceed them and you’re flagged permanently.

Safe rates:

  • Instagram: Max 60 actions/hour per account
  • Amazon: 1 request per 2-3 seconds
  • Google Search: 1 request per 5-10 seconds

Mistake #3: Ignoring Session Management

For account logins and checkouts: Use sticky sessions (10-30 minutes). Mid-session IP changes trigger fraud detection.

For large-scale scraping: Rotate aggressively (every 1-5 requests) to distribute traffic and avoid rate limits.

Quick Decision Matrix: Find Your Proxy in 30 Seconds

Scraping e-commerce at scale? → Rotating Residential (Bright Data, Decodo, Oxylabs)

Managing 50+ Instagram accounts? → Static ISP or Mobile, 1 per account (NetNut, OnlineProxy)

Tracking Google rankings in 20 cities? → Residential with city targeting (Bright Data, Oxylabs)

Running sneaker bots? → ISP Proxies (NetNut, Live Proxies)

Creating multiple SaaS accounts? → Rotating Residential (Decodo, IPRoyal)

Verifying mobile ads globally? → Mobile Proxies (OnlineProxy, SOAX)

The 2026 Reality: What’s Changed

AI-powered anti-bot systems now analyze behavioral patterns, not just IPs. Solution: Combine proxies with anti-detect browsers and realistic automation timing.

Mobile-first verification is mandatory for account creation on major platforms. Budget 20-30% more for mobile proxies than in 2024.

Ethical sourcing scrutiny is intensifying. Choose providers with transparent IP sourcing and GDPR compliance. Red flag: residential proxies under $1/GB (likely unethically obtained).

Your Action Plan: Start Today

Step 1 (15 min): Take inventory of your existing proxy usage. Questions to ask: Am I even using the right proxy type for each workflow? Could I be paying less?

Step 2 (30 min): Align each workflow with the proxy type we discussed above using this sheet.

Step 3 (1-2 weeks): Trial 2-3 providers using their free trials. Run YOUR workflows and record success rate, speed, pricing.

Step 4 (1 month): Begin implementation slowly and monitor usage weekly for the highest cost workflows. Make adjustments monthly.

Winning businesses in 2026 will not pay more for proxies than they did last year. They will pay intelligently! Stop throwing money at your proxy provider. Start by pairing proper proxy types to your workflows.

Want expert guidance on your specific proxy strategy? Contact Ziny’s proxy specialists for a free consultation and custom recommendation tailored to your exact workflows and budget.

The post Choosing Proxies for Each Workflow: Scraping, SMM, Monitoring, Multi-Login (One Page Guide) appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: Top Proxy Setups for Amazon, Walmart, and Target Scraping in 2026
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
Scrapping for Amazon, Walmart, and Target Scraping Amazon, Walmart and Target is no longer a “pet project”, it’s a necessity for data-driven decision-making at scale. In 2026, online retail is more competitive than ever. These three retailers alone power the largest product ecosystems on the web, and their bot-detection technology is becoming more sophisticated. Products that can therefore […]

The post Top Proxy Setups for Amazon, Walmart, and Target Scraping in 2026 appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
Scrapping for Amazon, Walmart, and Target

Scraping Amazon, Walmart and Target is no longer a “pet project”, it’s a necessity for data-driven decision-making at scale. In 2026, online retail is more competitive than ever. These three retailers alone power the largest product ecosystems on the web, and their bot-detection technology is becoming more sophisticated. Products that can therefore scrape multi-retailer data for real-time pricing, inventory changes, customer sentiment etc gain a priceless competitive advantage.

Lets take a closer look at how to architect scalable and ban-proof scraping systems for the top retailers. We will also explore why Ziny Proxy is the most trusted proxy infrastructure provider for large-volume scraping operations.

Why Multi-Retailer Scraping Matters

Prices, rank, availability and search visibility are more volatile than ever. Monitoring all three retailers helps you gain market visibility and competitive intelligence that you can’t get from looking at just one site.

  • Price Intelligence: Brands monitor price changes across Amazon, Walmart, Target and other channels to detect MAP violations, dynamically adjust their own pricing in real time and uncover margin opportunities. Prices on Amazon can change rapidly from algorithmic updates that factor in Buy Box status, Prime status, fulfillment method, seller rank, and reviews. Walmart is regional with its prices to combat local retailer pricing, while Target is more static but its prices are affected by its regional and retailer specific Circle membership discounts.
  • Inventory Insights: Scraping live inventory from all three channels surfaces supply health, restock cadence, out of stock heatmaps and other demand-driving signals for high volume SKUs and promotions.
  • Marketplace SEO & Search Visibility: The search algorithms of each retailer have different goals and priorities. Amazon’s A9 is conversion focused. Walmart is more balanced between relevance and inventory availability. Target is localized and personalized. Monitoring SEO health and performance on multiple retailers is key for surfacing changes in demand, competitive visibility and other sales opportunities.
  • Customer Reviews & Sentiment: Scraping reviews on each retailer provides unfiltered, raw customer insight from every retailer’s unique customer base. Target shoppers are typically in store customers, Walmart customers are price driven and Amazon’s audience is typically more technical.
  • Amazon Seller Intelligence: Scraping Amazon Seller Data uncovers rogue sellers, Buy Box shifts, and FBA vs FBM competition. This information is essential to power more competitive conversion strategies.

How Each Retailer’s Architecture Shapes Your Scraper

Each retailer has a unique technical architecture that creates specific opportunities and challenges for scraping.

  1. Amazon:
     Amazon is very JavaScript and API-centric, with a significant portion of product information loading asynchronously. Page templates dynamically change according to region, user, and test group, making scraping very challenging. In order to track Buy Box movements, Prime eligibility, and seller type changes, you’ll need to contend with multiple page layouts, subdomains, and often authenticated sessions.
  2. Walmart:
    Walmart uses a combination of server-side rendered HTML and JavaScript hydration. Product data is usually found within embedded JSON or an API endpoint that can be found through network inspection. Pricing and availability data is ZIP-targeted, so residential proxies are required for accuracy.
  3. Target:
    Target is completely API-driven and rendered on React with GraphQL endpoints powering all data. Pricing, inventory, and promotions change not only per store location, but also based on membership program (Circle, RedCard), introducing additional scraper complexity.

Common Anti-Bot Defenses:
At the time of writing, all three deploy multi-layered bot detection defenses to catch scraping activity:

  • IP reputation databases
  • CAPTCHA triggers
  • TLS fingerprinting (e.g. chloecompat, SSL version)
  • Behavior analysis (i.e. straight-line requests, navigation speed)
  • Device fingerprinting

But all of their defenses have the same objective: catch bots that don’t look and act human. To pass anti-bot detection, you have to pass all the defenses.

Building a Scalable Scraping System

Architecture is key to ensuring long-term scraper health. Scalable is the ability to expand without exponentially increasing the operational effort.

Request Management

Managing thousands of concurrent HTTP requests is a non-trivial systems engineering problem. What concurrency rate is safe for each retailer? How do you back off gracefully to avoid being rate-limited or blocked? How can we ensure our most high-value and timely data (prices, inventory) gets highest priority over updates that can afford delays (metadata, promotions)? Frameworks like Celery with RabbitMQ or Kafka for queue management can handle distributing requests across multiple scrapers.

Proxy Management

Smart proxy rotation, health checking, and session persistence are also critical for stability and anti-bot mitigation. How do we rotate smartly and how do we keep track of individual proxy success rates over time? It’s best to separate proxy pools by retailer, and also auto-retire underperforming proxies which trigger CAPTCHAs or have slow response times.

Parsing & Normalization

HTML parsing, data extraction, error recovery and normalization into a common schema (product IDs, titles, images, prices, attributes, etc) is a complex task given template and product detail differences among the three retailers. Adaptive parsing which can choose between extracting HTML vs. JSON or GraphQL endpoints is a key to long-term health.

Storage Strategy

Tiered storage is essential: fast databases for current pricing/inventory levels (PostgreSQL), time-series for price history, and object storage (S3, GCS) for raw page archives. This allows a balance between operational performance and cost while also preserving the option to reprocess older data.

Monitoring & Alerting

Monitoring every metric which can signal a scraper is under attack or unhealthy is essential: success rate, average CAPTCHA frequency, proxy average latency, bad-false negative rates on key fields, etc. Alerting early can often prevent cascading performance loss or broader bans. Real-time monitoring dashboards can make diagnosing new bans or site template changes much simpler.

Advanced Anti-Detection & Ban Survival

The most advanced parts of building a robust, long-lived scraper are actually acting and looking like a real user.

  1. Header & TLS Fingerprinting: Requests should have the same HTTP headers and TLS handshake (Chrome, Windows, etc.) as a real browser profile. curl-impersonate and Playwright stealth mode have better real-browser emulation than vanilla Python libraries.
  2. CAPTCHA Prevention: Scrapers can avoid CAPTCHAs with human-like speeds: 2–5 req/min/IP on Amazon, 3–8 on Walmart, 2–6 on Target, using intelligent proxy rotation and random delays. 99% of CAPTCHAs are due to bad proxies and/or too much concurrency.
  3. JavaScript Fingerprinting: Sites fingerprint automation using JavaScript information such as navigator.webdriver, WebGL, and canvas fingerprinting. Undetected-chromedriver and Puppeteer Stealth spoof these values and randomize fingerprints.
  4. Behavioral Mimicry: Scrolling, reading, random delays, clicks, cookies, and natural redirects mimic human behavior. The more human-like the scraper, the longer it will live.

Data Extraction Strategies That Actually Work

So your scraper works. Stable and reliable. You make a few optimizations. Efficient is the next step.

Leveraging Internal APIs:
APIs internal to retailers tend to be the most reliable place to grab data. They’re very much not public-facing, so to find these you need to turn to the Chrome dev tools and start hunting. We’ve found Amazon offer listing XMLs, Walmart item JSON endpoints and Target GraphQL queries, for example. APIs are not only usually much faster and more reliable than scraping HTML pages, they’re also immune to HTML layout changes.

Browser Automation for JS-Heavy Pages:
When no API is available, or the data you need requires JavaScript processing, it’s time to simulate a real user browsing and leverage a browser automation. Tool like Playwright or Puppeteer can help with page rendering, run JavaScript, and grabbing data.

Strategic Crawl Depth:
Scrape dynamic data like price, availability, and reviews daily, and scrape less dynamic categories like name, description, and categories weekly. The more time spent on retailer-specific behavior like Amazon’s variations, Walmart’s ZIP-level inventory tracking, and Target’s store inventory and Circle promotions the better.

Cross-Retailer Normalization:
Pull data with consistent product IDs and other common fields so your datasets are consistent across retailers, allowing you to more easily cross-compare and analyze data from multiple retailers.

Scaling for Speed and Reliability

Speed and efficiency are of utmost importance at scale. This requires the same diligence, but further engineering and distributed strategies.

  1. Parallelization & Distribution: Asynchronous I/O is your friend, as is a distributed set of worker nodes. Async libraries such as aiohttp or asyncio can help, as can frameworks like Scrapy for distributed task management. Assigning retailer queues and operating on retailer-specific infrastructure, which can be distributed geographically to geographically locate traffic sources closer to users, can provide critical performance gains.
  2. Smart Rate Limiting: Rate limiting algorithms such as token bucket or leaky bucket can impose natural-looking concurrency and session expiration times. Dynamic rate throttling which adapts automatically based on real-time “safe rate” signals (speed bumps and CAPTCHAs) is essential.
  3. Data Layer Optimization: Time-series optimized datastores such as TimescaleDB can more efficiently track high-velocity price change history, while regular SQL databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL) serve well for indexing and lookups. Cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery) can support complex retailer analytics at multi-terabyte scale.
  4. Comprehensive Monitoring: Tracking request rate, average response time, request success ratio, and proxy health is critical to scaling sustainably and catching performance regression or site updates. Alerting on early signs like a sharp increase in CAPTCHAs (aim to keep <2-3%), or on overall success rates falling below 80% is critical. Monitoring dashboards let teams visualize trends and anomalies from the console without needing to manually audit data. A rapid CAPTCHA increase on Target might indicate it’s time to acquire a fresh batch of IPs.

Getting the Backbone Right: Proxy Infrastructure

Your proxies are your best disguise. Smart rotation, rigorous testing, and session persistence are essential. Retailer-specific optimizations:

  • Amazon: Rotate with high-trust ISP residential IPs only, keep concurrency low (2-3 simultaneous connections per IP) and if scraping multi-step flows, use sticky sessions to preserve login through the entire flow.
  • Walmart: Use geo-targeted ZIP-level residential IPs from US-based ISPs only.
  • Target: UU residential IPs with good Store-level IP-to-region mapping for geo. Stable sessions for checkout/cart flows and Circle features.

Pool Management:
Divide your proxies into pools by retailer and major regions (like West US, East US, Europe). Track performance on a per-IP basis, and automate retirement or downgrading of underperforming IPs. Keep tiers of premium IPs reserved for sensitive targets or rotate those slower for broad load testing and other low-value jobs.

Why Ziny Proxy Leads the Pack in 2026

Ziny Proxy is built from the ground up for scalable and ban-proof scraping, particularly against large data volume projects like multi-retailer intelligence.

  • Unlimited Rotation: We do not artificially throttle requests on high-volume Ziny Proxy endpoints. No limits to concurrent requests or how fast we rotate between addresses.
  • Global Residential Coverage: Ziny Proxy also has the largest global coverage of ISP residential IPs across all major U.S. regions. Crucial for accurate geo-targeting and retail scraping.
  • Sticky & Rotating Sessions: We support sticky (session persistence) for multi-step processes or rotating for higher-speed scraping. You can even rotate slower between entire groups of IPs for even broader geographic distribution.
  • Low CAPTCHA Footprint: Our optimized routing network keeps our CAPTCHA rates under 1–2% on average which significantly improves scraper efficiency.
  • IP Health Monitoring: Each IP is actively and passively monitored for latency, success rate, and block frequencies. This guarantees consistency without operational overhead.
  • Developer-Friendly: Ziny Proxy’s backconnect endpoints can work with any library that can perform requests, so it’s friendly to Scrapy, Playwright, Puppeteer, custom HTTP clients, or other headless browsers.

We designed Ziny Proxy specifically for high-volume scraping use cases–especially multi-retailer data pulls–so it requires less effort to keep your operations running with reduced risk of downtime and bans.

Final Thoughts

Scraping Amazon, Walmart and Target will only become more sophisticated in the years to come. It’s no longer about building a clever script, but a resilient infrastructure with a layered approach to detection, robust request management, smart rate limiting, and most importantly proxy rotation which looks and acts human. Residential IPs, smart browser fingerprinting and behavioral mimicry are the new scraping standard.

Proxy infrastructure like Ziny Proxy combined with a mature monitoring strategy is how businesses and internal teams will maintain sustainable and compliant access to data from multi-retailer ecosystems on an operational basis.

The post Top Proxy Setups for Amazon, Walmart, and Target Scraping in 2026 appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: No-Code Scrapers + Ziny Setup: Your Step-by-Step Guide
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
No-Code Scrapers with Ziny Setup You absolutely need competitor prices, qualified lead lists, or in-depth market data to stay competitive in 2025. However, when you search for web scraping solutions, you get immediately overwhelmed. You could hire a developer for $8,000+/month, outsource to a scraping agency for $5,000+/project, or you can roll up your sleeves and learn […]

The post No-Code Scrapers + Ziny Setup: Your Step-by-Step Guide appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
No-Code Scrapers with Ziny Setup

You absolutely need competitor prices, qualified lead lists, or in-depth market data to stay competitive in 2025. However, when you search for web scraping solutions, you get immediately overwhelmed. You could hire a developer for $8,000+/month, outsource to a scraping agency for $5,000+/project, or you can roll up your sleeves and learn Python yourself. This may means weeks of tutorials and googling Stack Overflow errors before you get your first data point.

There is an easier way that levels the playing field for everyone. Scraping websites with no-code solutions like Apify, BrowserAct, and Outscraper along with Ziny’s residential proxies gives you enterprise-grade scraping capabilities in about 30 minutes flat. Ready to extract hundreds of thousands of data points for free this very second?

Choose Your Scraping Tool (5 Minutes)

The first step is to simply pick the tool you want to use. There are three fantastic options here that each excel in different use cases and learning curves. It’s mainly about finding the best fit for your needs right now.

Apify — The Power User’s Choice

Apify has the largest catalog of scrapers to date with over 1,500 pre-built scrapers ready to start extracting right now. It ranges from Google Maps to Instagram, LinkedIn to Amazon and many more. The tool excels when you want to chain together complex multi-step workflows. It can also create custom scrapers from scratch using the built-in visual editor. The tradeoff is a steeper learning curve vs the other options.

Choose Apify if you want:

  • 1,500+ pre-built scrapers for any website
  • Multi-step workflows, sophisticated data pipelines
  • Developer-level access through a public API
  • Enterprise-grade flexibility and configuration options
  • Automate scraping with scheduled runs

Skip Apify if you don’t want to spend any time learning or just need to scrape Google data.

Cost: $49-$499/month depending on usage + proxy costs
Free Trial: $5 in credits, no credit card required to start

BrowserAct — The Easiest Tool

BrowserAct is a modern web scraper that uses AI to completely remove technical hurdles. Instead of manually defining selectors and data schema, you write instructions in plain English. E.g “extract all the prices on this page” and the AI takes care of the rest automatically. The visual drag-and-drop workflow builder is incredibly intuitive and transparent even for the absolute beginner. Most impressively, the AI also dynamically adapts to changes on websites in the background so you no longer need to constantly maintain scrapers.

Choose BrowserAct if you want:

  • 100% natural language commands with zero technical setup
  • Visual no-code interface that’s completely intuitive
  • AI that automatically adapts when websites change
  • The absolute easiest learning curve possible for newbies
  • Visualize exactly what’s happening in real time

Skip BrowserAct if you’re strapped for cash or need high volume scraping across thousands of niche websites.

Cost: Credits-based system, typically 80-250 credits per scraping task
Free Trial: Available through AppSumo deals and promotional offers

Outscraper — The Google Specialist

Outscraper is different from the other tools in that it has carved out a very specific niche. It specializes in Google’s own platforms like Google Maps, Google Search, and Google Shopping. Outscraper is able to offer the most transparent and lowest cost per result. The main downside is scope since with only about 20 scrapers available you can’t use it for social media or general e-commerce scraping. But within its narrow use case, it’s the best in class in terms of both simplicity and cost.

Choose Outscraper if you want:

  • Google Maps businesses with verified email addresses
  • Google Search results to track and analyze for SEO
  • Google Shopping prices for monitoring and tracking
  • Pay-as-you-go pricing with no fixed monthly commitment
  • The absolute simplest user interface and minimal configuration

Skip Outscraper if you need to scrape social networks or general e-commerce sites.

Cost: Approximately $3 per 1,000 results extracted
Free Trial: 500 free results immediately upon signup

Set Up Ziny Proxies (5 Minutes)

Why Proxies Aren’t Optional

It is obvious to any website that you’re running an automated bot when your scraper hits hundreds or thousands of times. Especially from the exact same IP address in a short period. Residential proxies like Ziny solve this problem by routing your requests through different real residential IP addresses.

To the website you look like hundreds of thousands of genuine users browsing from all over the world. This is the fundamental difference between getting blocked in 5 minutes and successfully scraping millions of data points over several months without a hitch.

Types of Proxies in a Nutshell

Ziny offers three types of proxies to match your needs. Residential proxies offer the best balance of anonymity and success rates for most use cases. Mobile proxies enjoy the highest trust levels from websites at the expense of higher cost. Finally, datacenter proxies are the fastest but at the cost of detection (each has its place).

In general, residential proxies are your best choice for scraping projects. The others are best reserved for highly specialized use cases.

Step 1: Get Your Ziny Credentials

The setup process is very simple and takes under two minutes. 

Go to dashboard.ziny.io in your browser and complete the ultra fast 60-second signup process. You’ll be immediately logged into your dashboard and ready to go. Click on the “Premium Residential” tab in your dashboard navigation. You’ll see your proxy credentials prominently displayed.

Copy and paste the two credentials somewhere safe:

USERNAME
PASSWORD

Step 2: Understand Your Proxy Formats

The basic proxy format works on all platforms and scraping tools. It’s a standard HTTP proxy URL that combines your credentials with Ziny’s proxy server details. Below is the most commonly used proxy format:

http://YOUR_USERNAME:YOUR_PASSWORD@proxy.ziny.io:8000

The advanced feature that sets Ziny apart is the ability to target very specific locations with the proxies. When you need to extract location-specific data, regional pricing, etc., you must use geo-targeting. For Ziny, this is as simple as adding your desired country to the username like this:

http://YOUR_USERNAME-country-US:YOUR_PASSWORD@proxy.ziny.io:8000

Replace US with any 2-letter country code. UK for United Kingdom, DE for Germany, CA for Canada, AU for Australia, etc. across over 195 countries.

Ziny even offers multi-page scraping support with sticky sessions. This helps to maintain the exact same IP address for hours or days at a time. Sticky sessions is 100% required when scraping paginated results, or you need to keep a session alive for multiple requests. The format is:

http://YOUR_USERNAME-session-abc123:YOUR_PASSWORD@proxy.ziny.io:8000

Replace abc123 with any unique session ID you want. Ziny will keep the same IP for 24 hours or until you change the session ID.

Setup Guides for Each Platform

Apify Setup (12 Minutes)

Let’s build a scraper that extracts 100 coffee shops from Google Maps including business names, full addresses, phone numbers, star ratings, and total review counts. The exact same process applies to any Google Maps scraping use case.

Step-by-step process:

  1. Go to apify.com and click the big green “Sign up free” button at the top right. After confirming your email, you’ll get $5 of free credits. Log in and visit the “Store” in the top navigation bar to access Apify’s marketplace of over 1,500 pre-built scrapers. Search for “Google Maps Scraper” and click the official one created by Apify themselves (it will have a verified Apify badge). Click “Try for free” to launch the configuration UI.
  2. In the basic settings section, paste your search query exactly as you would type it into Google Maps itself. In this example we’ll use coffee shops in the San Francisco area. Specify 100 max results for testing and select output format.
  3. Scroll down to “Proxy Configuration” and click the dropdown arrow. Choose “Custom proxies” and in the input box that appears, paste your Ziny proxy URL with geo-targeting.
  4. Hit the big “Start” button. Apify will launch a cloud browser instance that routes all requests through Ziny’s residential proxies. You’ll see real-time status updates including how many results have been extracted and the current success rate. The entire scrape should finish in 2-5 minutes.
  5. Click the “Dataset” tab to see the results. Click “Export” and select “Download CSV” to get your full dataset with all the structured data ready for immediate use.

BrowserAct Setup (10 Minutes)

In this example we’re going to build an AI scraper that extracts product information from any e-commerce site using nothing but plain English.

Step-by-step process:

1. Go to browseract.com and sign up using your email. You’ll get a confirmation email immediately – this is just to verify the account is really yours. The UI itself is purpose-built for absolute beginners who have never written a line of code.

2. Click the “+ Create” button you’ll see prominently in the center of the dashboard. Name your agent something recognizable like “Amazon Price Scraper” so you can identify it later when you have multiple agents active.

3. Before writing scraping instructions, click “Browser Settings” in the left sidebar. This is the absolutely crucial step that most users miss entirely. For Region, choose “United States” or whatever primary audience country your target website serves. Choose “Residential” for IP Type to ensure you use Ziny’s most reliable proxies. Toggle “Proxy Configuration” on and paste your Ziny URL with geo-targeting.

http://YOUR_USERNAME-country-US:YOUR_PASSWORD@proxy.ziny.io:8000

4. Now type your scraping instructions in casual conversational English in the large textbox:

  • Visit [paste your Amazon product URL here]
  • Extract the product title
  • Extract the current price
  • Extract the star rating
  • Extract the total number of reviews
  • Export everything to a CSV file. When complete, simply click “Run Agent” and watch in the browser UI as the scraper agent executes your commands in real time: clicking elements, scrolling down, extracting data exactly as you told it to. Once finished, click “Export” and “CSV” to download the results.

Outscraper Setup (8 Minutes)

In this example we’re going to extract 500 local businesses from Google Maps with verified email addresses included– perfect for B2B lead generation or local market research.

Step-by-step process:

1. Visit outscraper.com and go through the signup process. You’ll get 500 free results immediately so you can validate the quality before committing to a paid plan. Importantly, no credit card is required upfront so you can try it out risk-free.

2. Click the “Services” menu in the main navigation bar and scroll down to find “Google Maps Email Extractor”. The “Email Extractor” not only pulls business data but also finds and verifies email addresses associated with those businesses. Click “Start Scraping” to open the configuration page.

3. In the search query field, paste the exact search you would type in Google Maps. In this example, we’ll scrape restaurants in Chicago. Set the maximum results to 500 so you make full use of your free trial. Language is already set to English and the country to United States.

4. Scroll all the way down to the “Advanced Settings” section and toggle “Proxy Settings” on. In the text box that appears, paste your Ziny URL.

5. Click the big “Extract” button and wait 3-5 minutes. When complete, click “Download” and select either CSV or XLSX to receive structured data with business names, addresses, phone numbers, verified emails, websites, and star ratings.

The end result is waking up to already updated pricing intelligence every single morning with zero manual work. You see your competitors change prices before they gain market share, and you can adjust your prices in response first.

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Problem: “Proxy Authentication Failed”

If you see this error message, it means your scraper cannot connect to Ziny’s servers with your credentials. Go back to dashboard.ziny.io and recopy your username and password very carefully. Ensure there are no extra spaces before or after. Ensure you have the correct format:

http://username:password@proxy.ziny.io:8000

Problem: Success Rate Below 80%

Your scraper finishes with a low success rate, meaning the website has partially detected your bot. Add sticky session to your proxy username to keep same IP for whole scrape (-session-abc123). Lower your speed to 5-10 requests per minute rather than flooding the website. Match the proxy country with the target website’s primary region.

Problem: Missing Data Fields

Some fields are blank even though the scraper completed successfully. Increase wait time to 3-5 seconds so JS has time to load dynamic content. Turn on JS rendering in the scraper’s advanced settings. Check to see if the website recently changed their layout or design.

Problem: Burning Credits Too Fast

You notice you are using credits way too fast than anticipated because of inefficient setup. Lower your max results to 10-20 during testing then scale up later. Paginate intelligently instead of loading everything at once. Filter during the scrape, don’t scrape everything and filter later.
